- `#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.794d` — request-party-member-stats represented visible aura snapshot. Source-of-truth: C++ `PartyMemberStats::Write` writes `Auras.size()` before phases/chromie and serializes each `PartyMemberAuraStates` after `CTROptions` as `SpellID`, `Flags`, `ActiveFlags`, `Points.size()` and point floats (`/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Server/Packets/PartyPackets.cpp:163-172,201-247`); `PartyMemberFullState::Initialize(Player const*)` fills those fields from `player->GetVisibleAuras()` using `AuraApplication::GetBase()->GetId()`, `GetEffectMask()` and `GetFlags()`, adding scalable effect amounts only when `AFLAG_SCALABLE` is set (`PartyPackets.cpp:642-663`; layout in `PartyPackets.h:158-165`). Rust now has `PartyMemberAuraState`, writes aura count and per-aura wire fields in C++ order, preserves `AuraApplication::effect_mask` for represented auras, publishes sorted visible aura snapshots through `PlayerBroadcastInfo`, and `RequestPartyMemberStats` copies them into `PartyMemberFullState`. Boundary: scalable `Points` remain empty because Rust `AuraApplication` does not yet retain full `AuraEffect` amount snapshots; pet aura stats, vehicle seat id, dungeon score, full category/runtime validation and live client/bot/manual validation remain open.
- `#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.794c` — request-party-member-stats canonical power snapshot. Source-of-truth: C++ `PartyMemberFullState::Initialize(Player const*)` writes `PowerType = player->GetPowerType()`, `CurrentPower = player->GetPower(player->GetPowerType())`, and `MaxPower = player->GetMaxPower(player->GetPowerType())` (`/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Server/Packets/PartyPackets.cpp:619-624`), with `Unit::GetPower` / `GetMaxPower` returning zero when the power index is invalid (`/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Entities/Unit/Unit.cpp:9269-9285`). Rust `WorldSession::canonical_player_party_power_snapshot_like_cpp` now reads canonical `UnitData::display_power` and the canonical player's current/max power for that power type, publishing those values through `PlayerBroadcastInfo` instead of class-derived type plus zero placeholders. Fallback remains class-derived type + zero only when no canonical player snapshot is available. Remaining gaps: vehicle seat id, scalable aura points, pet stats, dungeon score, full category/runtime validation and live client/bot/manual validation remain open.
- `#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.794b` — request-party-member-stats represented status-bit fidelity. Remaining gaps: vehicle seat id, scalable aura points, pet stats, dungeon score, full category/runtime validation and live client/bot/manual validation remain open.
- `#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.794a` — request-party-member-stats player snapshot fidelity. Superseded by `#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.794b` for the remaining represented status bits. Superseded by `#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.794c` for canonical power and `#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.794d` for represented visible auras. - `#NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031j30` — WotLK spellclick represented `CreatureAI::OnSpellClick` evidence (not manual-test-ready; no server/bot validation). Rust now records bounded represented AI evidence on the clicked `Creature` after at least one represented spellclick cast executes: `CreatureAiOwnershipState.last_spell_click_inform = { clicker, spell_click_handled: true }`, routed through the legacy `WorldCreature` when present and through the canonical creature record otherwise. `RepresentedSpellClickExecutionOutcomeLikeCpp` now reports `ai_on_spell_click_represented` and keeps `ai_on_spell_click_unrepresented` when skipped/failed/unrepresented cast rails mean the final C++ handled boolean cannot be proven. Boundaries: no SmartScript `ProcessEventsFor(SMART_EVENT_ON_SPELLCLICK)` dispatch, no full AI enabled/type gate beyond represented creature evidence, no vehicle seat/basepoint/aura fallback, no pet/vehicle caster rail beyond current represented rows, no multi-session fanout, no live client/bot/manual validation.

Focused tests cover the no-context no-op, positive hover/fall launch, underwater guard, and root guard. Remaining gaps: world/map caller still must provide real terrain height / underwater state, live `MoveSplineInit::SetFall` packet/fanout is not wired from death yet, and no live server/client/bot validation was run.
- `SCOPE-CORRECTION / #NEXT.SCOPE.WOTLK.002` (BlackMarket frozen outside WotLK scope; no progress credit for WotLK) — Black Market Auction House is a MoP-era system, not WotLK gameplay. `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy` contains `BlackMarketMgr`, handler, config, and packet surfaces because the reference tree includes forward-ported/modern systems, but this is not active WotLK port scope. Preserve all docs/inventory references for future-version coverage and compatibility review, but do not select BlackMarket implementation slices for WotLK progress, do not count BlackMarket work toward the honest WotLK percentage, and keep any existing opcode/config surface dormant, no-op, or explicitly rejected unless the target version changes.
- `SCOPE-CORRECTION / #NEXT.SCOPE.WOTLK.001` (BattlePets frozen outside WotLK scope; no progress credit for WotLK) — BattlePets/Pet Battles were introduced in MoP-era gameplay and are not a WotLK gameplay system. The forward-ported `BattlePetMgr` surface in `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy` is preserved as C++ reference only for compatibility/modern-client baggage, not as active WotLK port scope. Recent represented BattlePet work (including uncage, journal, slot, summon, name, error/update packet seams) is retained in Rust for a possible future MoP+/modern-client target, but from this point it is frozen: do not pick new BattlePet slices for WotLK progress, do not use BattlePet slices to raise the honest WotLK percentage, and keep any existing runtime behavior dormant/compat-only unless the target version explicitly changes. For WotLK pets, continue with the real WotLK systems instead: hunter/warlock/minion pets, stable/pet spells, cosmetic critter/companion summon, item/spell learning paths that exist in WotLK, and live runtime AI/combat/maps.

Important fidelity note: despite the name and handler comment, this Trinity branch does **not** check that the target is mechanical, so Rust does not fabricate a mechanical-type gate. Rust now exposes the constant and executes represented hydrated rows plus empty-`effects[]` primary fallbacks by delegating the positive amount to the existing represented `apply_heal` player/creature path. - `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031f0` (represented direct heal/damage C++ guard correction; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74-75%) — re-contrasted C++ `Spell::EffectHeal` (`SpellEffects.cpp:1167-1212`), `Spell::EffectSchoolDMG` (`SpellEffects.cpp:500-548`), and post-effect heal/damage application (`Spell.cpp:2865-2925`). C++ only adds heal/damage when the target is alive, returns before healing when `damage < 0`, and only applies damage when `m_damage > 0`; Rust previously converted negative `effect_base_points` with `as u32`, allowing huge represented heal/damage, and `apply_heal` could revive a dead represented player. Rust now skips negative represented `SPELL_EFFECT_HEAL` / `SPELL_EFFECT_SCHOOL_DAMAGE` amounts, and represented heal/damage no-op for dead creature targets before mutating health or sending update packets; self-heal also no-ops for dead represented players. Rust had accidentally encoded `SPELL_EFFECT_HEAL = 6` and `SPELL_EFFECT_APPLY_AURA = 35`, which crossed normal aura application with party-area-aura ids and made vehicle heal immunity use the wrong effect id. Rust now exposes the C++ ids in `wow-data`, vehicle immunity uses effect 10 for heal, and live represented spell dispatch matches by named constants instead of magic numeric literals. - `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031ey` (bounded represented-player `SPELL_EFFECT_ENVIRONMENTAL_DAMAGE` consumer; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74-75%) — contrasted C++ `Spell::EffectEnvironmentalDMG` (`SpellEffects.cpp:468-493`), spell effect id `SPELL_EFFECT_ENVIRONMENTAL_DAMAGE = 7` (`SharedDefines.h:1153`), and the player branch into `Player::EnvironmentalDamage(DAMAGE_FIRE, damage)` (`Player.cpp:621-670`). Rust now exposes `SPELL_EFFECT_ENVIRONMENTAL_DAMAGE` in `wow-data`, and live represented spell execution applies that effect only for the represented player target when alive: it subtracts the effect's per-effect base damage, syncs represented player state, sends owner `SMSG_HEALTH_UPDATE` when health changes, then sends `SMSG_ENVIRONMENTAL_DAMAGE_LOG` with type `DAMAGE_FIRE` and zero represented absorb/resist. Coverage proves `SpellGo -> HealthUpdate -> EnvironmentalDamageLog -> CooldownEvent` order and validates the combat-log payload. Rust now carries represented spell-focus template evidence for `SPELL_FOCUS` / `UI_LINK` GameObjects, searches the current canonical map's nearby GameObject cells by focus type, spawned state, and 3D radius, and uses the found `focusObject` to gate live GameObject summons. Wild summons now use the focus object's position/orientation as the fallback close-point target when no explicit destination is supplied; slot summons use focus only as the C++ cast gate. Tests cover template data indices, lifecycle source carry, focus search rejection/acceptance, missing-focus no fabricated GO, and focus-backed live wild summon visibility/position. Scope remains bounded: no `SPELL_AURA_PROVIDE_SPELL_FOCUS` aura bypass, no full `Spell::CheckCast` failure packet path, no complete implicit target preparation, no exact TC grid traversal/phase parity beyond represented map visibility, no GO object-size close-point reach for focus objects, no scripts/linked-trap summon side effects, no multi-session fanout claim, and no manual client/server validation.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031ei` (live spell-loop wiring for non-focus `EffectSummonObjectWild` / `EffectSummonObject`; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74-75%) — contrasted C++ `Spell::EffectSummonObjectWild` (`SpellEffects.cpp:2937-2986`), `Spell::EffectSummonObject` (`SpellEffects.cpp:3541-3597`), `Spell::CheckCast` focus gate (`Spell.cpp:5951-5956`), `Spell::SearchSpellFocus` (`Spell.cpp:2308-2316`), and focus target preparation (`Spell.cpp:1141-1158`). Rust now detects the represented GameObject summon effects in `WorldSession::execute_spell_with_visual_and_target_data`: spells without `RequiresSpellFocus` call the already-audited session resolvers, and successful map-owned creations force a represented visibility refresh so the casting session can receive the canonical GO create/update. Spells with `RequiresSpellFocus != 0` are deliberately skipped until `SearchSpellFocus` / `focusObject` is ported, avoiding the false caster-based target/map/orientation that C++ would not use. Tests cover wild summon create+visibility, slotted summon create+slot+visibility, and focus-required guard/no fabricated GO. Scope remains bounded: no `SearchSpellFocus`, no focusObject destination/orientation/map selection, no cast-failure packet for missing focus, no scripts/linked traps/battleground state, no multi-session fanout claim, and no manual client/server validation.

- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031e0` (capture-point removed opcode audit; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74-75%) — contrasted `SMSG_CAPTURE_POINT_REMOVED` and `SMSG_UPDATE_CAPTURE_POINT` against legacy C++ `Opcodes.h:1920/2152` plus archived TrinityCore `Opcodes.h:1779/1916`. Both sources leave these battleground packets as `0xBADD`/`UNKNOWN_OPCODE`; Rust cannot expose duplicate `ServerOpcodes` discriminants, so `CapturePointRemoved` remains a distinct packet type with the C++ GUID-only payload while sharing the current `0xBADD` placeholder. This removes the misleading "confirm non-placeholder opcode" TODO from the port plan: a real client opcode would require external sniff/opcode-table research, not C++ port work. Remaining gaps stay functional: broader ObjectAccessor/cell traversal exactness, DB save/delete effects, and live client/server validation.

Because the legacy opcode table still marks both update and removed capture-point packets as `0xBADD`, the Rust packet intentionally reuses `ServerOpcodes::UpdateCapturePoint` while keeping a distinct payload type. - `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031ds` (canonical remove-list same-pass linked-trap drain; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74-75%) — contrasted against C++ `Map::RemoveAllObjectsInRemoveList`, which loops while the remove set is non-empty (`Map.cpp:2574-2646`), and `GameObject::RemoveFromWorld`, which calls `GetLinkedTrap()->DespawnOrUnsummon()` before object-store removal (`GameObject.cpp:926-948`). Rust now processes `objects_to_remove` until empty instead of draining a snapshot, and canonical linked-trap cleanup routes the trap through `gameobject_delete_like_cpp`/remove-list rather than direct physical removal. Focused tests assert direct `remove_from_map_like_cpp` leaves linked traps queued, while `remove_all_objects_in_remove_list_like_cpp` removes both owner and newly queued trap in the same call. - `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031d5` (canonical typed GameObject visibility create fallback; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74-75%) — contrasted against C++ `Map::AddToMap<T>` adding objects to the live grid/runtime and calling `AddToWorld` + `UpdateObjectVisibilityOnCreate` (`Map.cpp:530-574`), and `GameObject::Create` / `GameObject::AddToWorld` preserving map-owned create state and object-store registration (`GameObject.cpp:899-970`). Rust `visible_gameobjects_from_canonical_map_like_cpp` now falls back to the typed canonical `GameObject` record when no per-session represented GameObject state exists, while still honoring represented per-player despawn state and represented-state overrides when present. `GameObject` now preserves local create rotation beside the packed rotation so `GameObjectCreateData` can be rebuilt from canonical lifecycle data. Focused tests cover lifecycle rotation preservation, canonical typed GameObject create-data fallback, and the existing canonical cell scan regression. Still open: full GameObject runtime/use/scripts/traps/GO AI, complete update-value fanout, dynamic-tree/collision/object accessor parity, and live server/client validation.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031d4` (direct session DB creature query hydrates addon `PathId` + effective `MovementType`; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74-75%) — contrasted against C++ `Creature::LoadFromDB` using DB `CreatureData::movementType` (`Creature.cpp:1861`), `Creature::Create`/`LoadCreaturesAddon` applying addon data (`Creature.cpp:674`, `Creature.cpp:2734-2775`), `ObjectMgr::LoadCreatureAddons` downgrading spawn waypoint movement when a spawn-specific addon has `PathId=0`, and `WaypointMovementGenerator<Creature>::DoInitialize` resolving `owner->GetWaypointPathId()` (`WaypointMovementGenerator.cpp:120-148`). Rust `SEL_CREATURES_IN_RANGE` now appends an effective DB movement column plus `COALESCE(creature_addon.PathId, creature_template_addon.PathId, 0)`, preserving the existing first 35 columns/parameters. Both direct session DB visibility paths (`send_nearby_creatures` and `update_visibility`) pass those values into registration; the legacy/canonical compatibility creature now sets the represented default movement and nonzero waypoint path before `WorldCreature::from_canonical`, so DB waypoint creatures initialize the stored waypoint generator via the already-injected `WaypointPathStoreLikeCpp` resolver. Focused tests cover SQL shape/placeholder count, runtime default-movement sync, and legacy registration hydrating path+generator. Still open: live server/client validation, real `WaypointReached`/`WaypointPathEnded` AI dispatch, full path/MMAP generation and formation side effects, plus GameObject canonical visibility/runtime parity.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031d3` (internal loaded-grid AddToMap records mirrored to legacy runtime; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global moves only marginally, ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `Map::DoRespawn` creating DB-backed Creature/GameObject only on loaded grids (`Map.cpp:2165-2188`), `Map::SpawnGroupSpawn` loading DB objects and retaining the spawned pointer (`Map.cpp:2356-2395`), `PoolGroup<Creature>::Spawn1Object` using loaded-grid `CreateCreatureFromDB` (`PoolMgr.cpp:355-363`), and C++ `Map::AddToMap<T>` putting the object into the single live runtime (`Map.cpp:530-570`). Rust `wow-map` still does not depend on `wow-world`; instead, successful internal loaded-grid `AddToMap` seams now return cloned primary `MapObjectRecord`s through `ProcessRespawnsSafeSideEffectsSummaryLikeCpp` and `SpawnGroupSpawnOutcomeLikeCpp`. `world-server` consumes those records after map mutation and mirrors Creature records into the legacy `MapManager` via the existing canonical-to-legacy helper. This covers canonical tick respawn, `UpdateSpawnGroupConditions` spawn, pool/event-pool loaded-grid execution, and the prior direct game-event path. Focused tests cover `UpdateSpawnGroupConditions` internal loaded-grid Creature -> legacy mirror plus existing `wow-map` loaded-grid regression set. - `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031d2` (loaded-grid game-event Creature mirror into legacy runtime; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `Map::AddToMap<T>` adding the loaded object to the single live map runtime (`Map.cpp:530-570`), `Map::ProcessRespawns` / `DoRespawn` ownership (`Map.cpp:2191+`), `Creature::LoadCreaturesAddon` copying nonzero addon `PathId` into `_waypointPathId` (`Creature.cpp:2734-2773`), and `WaypointMovementGenerator<Creature>::DoInitialize` resolving that path (`WaypointMovementGenerator.cpp:120-148`). Rust split-runtime gap closed for the `world-server` game-event loaded-grid path: when a DB-backed loaded-grid `MapObjectRecord::Creature` is successfully added to the canonical `wow_map::Map`, `world-server` now mirrors the same canonical creature into the legacy `wow_world::MapManager` using canonical create-data reconstruction and the loaded `WaypointPathStoreLikeCpp` resolver. - `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031d0` (waypoint movement tick emits `MonsterMove`; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `WaypointMovementGenerator<Creature>::DoUpdate` running from the creature update (`WaypointMovementGenerator.cpp:208-244`) and `WaypointMovementGenerator<Creature>::StartMove` launching `MoveSplineInit` for the current node (`WaypointMovementGenerator.cpp:309-422`), whose launch is broadcast as movement to visible clients. Rust now retains addon `PathId` in `CreatureAddonLifecycleRecordLikeCpp` and applies the spawn-specific load-time rule at the loaded-grid movement selection seam: a concrete spawn with `WAYPOINT_MOTION_TYPE` and a spawn-specific `creature_addon.PathId == 0` selects `IDLE_MOTION_TYPE`. Template addon rows do not trigger this mutation, matching C++. Since `wow-entities` still only represents idle movement at the final entity seam, this is not waypoint runtime support; waypoint path execution/data loading and full motion generator parity remain open.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031bk` (DB-backed represented creature addon loading; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `ObjectMgr::LoadCreatureTemplateAddons` / `LoadCreatureAddons` (`ObjectMgr.cpp:766-897`, `ObjectMgr.cpp:1224-1367`) and `Creature::GetCreatureAddon` / `LoadCreaturesAddon` (`Creature.cpp:2722-2809`). Rust now loads `Emotes.db2`, then loads `creature_addon` and `creature_template_addon` into `CreatureAddonStoreLikeCpp` using the C++ selected columns, filters missing spawn/template owners, validates represented mount/emote/stand fields, and feeds the store into loaded-grid creature respawn caches. Scope remains represented: path runtime, auras, visual flags, anim tier/kits, sheath, pet flags, shapeshift, visibility distance override, hover, full validation reporting, and live client/server manual test remain open.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031bj` (loaded-grid creature addon resolver wiring; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `Creature::GetCreatureAddon` / `Creature::LoadCreaturesAddon` (`Creature.cpp:2722-2809`). `build_loaded_grid_creature_inputs_from_db_like_cpp` now accepts `CreatureAddonStoreLikeCpp`, resolves spawn-addon-before-template-addon into `ResolvedCreatureTemplateLikeCpp`, and `CreatureLoadedGridLifecycleResolverLikeCpp` passes the resolved addon into `CreatureCreateLifecycleRecord` so the represented mount/stand/PvP/emote subset is applied by lifecycle create/respawn. - `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031bi` (represented creature addon store fallback exists, dormant; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `ObjectMgr::LoadCreatureTemplateAddons` / `LoadCreatureAddons` (`ObjectMgr.cpp:766-897`, `ObjectMgr.cpp:1224-1367`) and `Creature::GetCreatureAddon` (`Creature.cpp:2722-2732`). Rust now exposes `CreatureAddonStoreLikeCpp` with spawn-addon-before-template-addon fallback, filters rows by caller-provided spawn/template existence predicates, normalizes supported mount/emote/stand fields like C++, and preserves the full PvP flag byte. - `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031bh` (represented creature addon local fields now apply on create and respawn; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `Creature::GetCreatureAddon` / `Creature::LoadCreaturesAddon` (`Creature.cpp:2722-2809`) and `CreatureAddon` (`CreatureData.h:677-694`). Rust now has an optional `CreatureAddonLifecycleRecordLikeCpp` on `CreatureCreateLifecycleRecord`, stores it in lifecycle metadata, applies represented mount display, stand state, PvP flags, and non-zero emote on lifecycle creation, and reapplies the same represented subset during `JustRespawned`. Scope remains deliberately bounded: no DB-backed `creature_addon` / `creature_template_addon` loading yet, no spawn-vs-template fallback, no path id, auras, visual flags, anim tier/kits, sheath, pet flags, shapeshift form, visibility distance override, hover, or addon validation.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031bg` (loaded-grid creature flags now honor spawn-row overrides from C++ `ChooseCreatureFlags`; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `ObjectMgr::ChooseCreatureFlags` (`ObjectMgr.cpp:1683-1697`) and `ObjectMgr::LoadCreatures` nullable `creature.npcflag`, `creature.unit_flags`, `creature.unit_flags2`, `creature.unit_flags3` loading (`ObjectMgr.cpp:2174-2229`, `CreatureData.h:643-646`). Rust now carries those nullable spawn-row overrides in `CreatureSpawnRuntimeRowLikeCpp`, reads them from the existing `SEL_CREATURE_SPAWNS` column positions, and `build_loaded_grid_creature_inputs_from_db_like_cpp` applies `runtime override -> template field` selection for npc/unit flags. This closes the bounded data-selection part of `ChooseCreatureFlags`; condition evaluation outside this explicit nullable source selection and addon reload remain open.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031bf` (game-event NPC flag live update now ORs active event overlay onto DB-backed template `npcflag`; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `GameEventMgr::UpdateEventNPCFlags` (`GameEventMgr.cpp:1115-1148`) and `GameEventMgr::GetNPCFlag` (`GameEventMgr.cpp:920-933`): the live update route gathers affected spawn ids from `game_event_npcflag`, computes active-event overlay via `GetNPCFlag(creature)`, and ORs `creatureTemplate->npcflag` when available. This live route is not gated by `CREATURE_FLAG_EXTRA_WORLDEVENT`; that gate appears in the respawn `Creature::setDeathState(JUST_RESPAWNED)` path (`Creature.cpp:2268-2284`). Rust's live `game_event_update_npc_flags_like_cpp` no longer applies overlay against zero; it looks up the spawn template in `CreatureTemplateLifecycleStoreLikeCpp`, ORs `template.npc_flags` with `CanonicalSpawnMetadataLikeCpp::game_event_npc_flag_mask_like_cpp`, and keeps the missing-template counter only for true missing templates. Scope remains bounded: full `ChooseCreatureFlags` condition evaluation, creature-data overrides, and reconciling the respawn-vs-live gate nuance remain open.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031be` (DB-backed creature template base npc/unit flags loaded and applied; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `Creature::setDeathState(JUST_RESPAWNED)` `ObjectMgr::ChooseCreatureFlags` / `ReplaceAllNpcFlags*` / `ReplaceAllUnitFlags*` (`Creature.cpp:2268-2284`), `ObjectMgr::LoadCreatureTemplate` column mapping (`ObjectMgr.cpp:349-375`, `ObjectMgr.cpp:403-430`), and world-event overlay source (`GameEventMgr.cpp:920-933`). Rust now loads `creature_template.npcflag`, `unit_flags`, `unit_flags2`, and `unit_flags3`, carries them through DB-backed loaded-grid lifecycle creation, seeds `CreatureAiOwnershipState`, and respawn reloads the represented template baseline. Scope remains bounded: full `ChooseCreatureFlags` condition evaluation, creature-data overrides, addon reload, and `CREATURE_FLAG_EXTRA_WORLDEVENT` overlay remain open; existing `game_event_update_npc_flags_like_cpp` still records the missing template-base gap, so event overlay is a follow-up slice.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031bd` (represented creature death now releases spell focus and cancels delayed reacquire; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `Creature::setDeathState(JUST_DIED)` (`Creature.cpp:2223-2224`) plus `SetSpellFocus` / `ReleaseSpellFocus` / `ReacquireSpellFocusTarget` / `DoNotReacquireSpellFocusTarget` (`Creature.cpp:3263-3389`, `Creature.h:495-502`). Rust now has represented `_spellFocusInfo` state on `Creature`, preserves the C++ delayed snapback shape (`withDelay=false` -> `Delay=1` for non-pets), clears `UNIT_STATE_FOCUSING` for represented `AI_DOESNT_FACE_TARGET` focus spells, and death runs `ReleaseSpellFocus(nullptr,false)` followed by `DoNotReacquireSpellFocusTarget()` before clearing the live target. Scope remains represented: real `Spell*` identity, `SpellInfo` attrs/aura gates, target-facing/ObjectAccessor restore, pet override timing, cast packet fanout, and full spell runtime are still open.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031bc` (DB-backed creature sparring health reload now preserves C++ float percentages; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `ObjectMgr::LoadCreatureTemplateSparring` / `GetCreatureTemplateSparringValues` / `Creature::LoadCreaturesSparringHealth` (`ObjectMgr.cpp:899-937`, `ObjectMgr.cpp:1468-1471`, `Creature.cpp:2799-2802`) and the call sites from `UpdateEntry`, `LoadFromDB`, home movement finalize, and respawn (`Creature.cpp:675`, `Creature.cpp:1141`, `HomeMovementGenerator.cpp:152`, `Creature.cpp:2292`). Rust now loads `creature_template_sparring`, skips missing-template and invalid `<=0` / `>100` rows like C++, stores percentages as `f32` instead of the previous lossy `u8`, applies a map-owned random selected value to loaded-grid creature lifecycle records, and keeps represented sparring damage/fake-damage using the float threshold. `MoveFall()` remains open deliberately: C++ gates it on `!IsUnderWater()` and real MotionMaster ground-height/spline execution, so implementing it in pure entity state now would invent behavior.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031as` (represented creature respawn now clears `UNIT_STATE_ALL_ERASABLE`; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `Creature::setDeathState(JUST_RESPAWNED)` (`Creature.cpp:2262`) and `UNIT_STATE_ALL_ERASABLE` (`Unit.h:283-296`): Rust now defines `UnitState::ALL_ERASABLE = ALL_STATE_SUPPORTED & !IGNORE_PATHFINDING` and clears it during `JustRespawned`, preserving `IGNORE_PATHFINDING` as C++ does.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031ar` (represented creature death now clears active state; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `Creature::setDeathState(JUST_DIED)` (`Creature.cpp:2227`): C++ calls `setActive(false)`. Rust already emitted a `Deactivate` runtime action but left represented `WorldObject.active` true; `Creature::set_death_state_runtime(JustDied)` now clears it directly. Scope remains partial: map active-list removal/fanout is still runtime-owned.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031aq` (represented creature respawn now clears corpse dynamic/unit flags; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `Unit::setDeathState(JUST_RESPAWNED)` (`Unit.cpp:8573-8574`) and `Creature::setDeathState(JUST_RESPAWNED)` (`Creature.cpp:2279-2284`): Rust now clears represented corpse dynamic flags with `replace_all_dynamic_flags(0)` and removes `SKINNABLE`/`IN_COMBAT` during `JustRespawned`. Scope remains partial: full `ChooseCreatureFlags` template reload, flags2/3, melee damage school, addon reload, and motion initialization remain open.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031ap` (represented creature death now interrupts non-melee spells with the C++ guard; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `Unit::setDeathState` (`Unit.cpp:8538-8539`) and `IsNonMeleeSpellCast`/`InterruptNonMeleeSpells` (`Unit.cpp:3050-3102`): Rust now uses a represented `is_non_melee_spell_cast_like_cpp(false, false, false, true)` guard before `interrupt_non_melee_spells(None, false, true)`, preserving the C++ edge case where a pure generic instant spell alone does not trigger the death interrupt path. Scope remains represented only: packet sends, cooldown/failure semantics, and full spell runtime remain open.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031ao` (represented creature death-state now normalizes remaining simple `JUST_DIED` fields; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `Unit::setDeathState` (`Unit.cpp:8552-8567`): after `CombatStop`, C++ clears reactives/diminishings and forces health 0, active power 0, emote none, and stand state stand. Rust now applies the represented subset in `Creature::set_death_state_runtime(JustDied)`. Scope remains intentionally partial: spell interruption, vehicle/totem/controlled cleanup, death-persistent aura filtering, movement `StopOnDeath`, ZoneScript/InstanceScript callbacks, and packet fanout remain open.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031an` (represented creature death-state now clears combat/threat/attackers like C++ `CombatStop`; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `Unit::setDeathState` (`Unit.cpp:8527-8554`): for non-alive states C++ calls `CombatStop()` before `JUST_DIED` side effects. Rust's represented `Creature::set_death_state_runtime` already cleared target/attacking and corpse/respawn state, but left represented threat refs and attacker sets alive. It now calls represented combat cleanup during `JustDied`; spell interrupts, vehicle/totem/controlled removal, aura-on-death removal, and full combat packet fanout remain open.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031am` (global legacy creature melee now mirrors canonical Creature victim hit/death state back into the legacy creature when both runtimes contain the victim; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `Unit::DealDamage`/`Unit::Kill` (`Unit.cpp:942-1016`, `Unit.cpp:10457-10614`): C++ has one `Creature` object, so health/death state cannot diverge between two managers. Rust's temporary dual-model runtime now records `legacy_creature_victim_syncs` and mirrors successful canonical Creature victim hits into the legacy `WorldCreature` on the same `(map, instance)`, including `JustDied -> Corpse` for lethal hits. This is a convergence guard for the current split, not final single-source ownership; loot/reward/proc/aura cleanup gaps remain open.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031al` (global legacy creature melee now promotes lethal canonical Creature victim hits through represented death state; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `Unit::DealDamage`, `Unit::Kill`, and `Creature::setDeathState(JUST_DIED)` (`Unit.cpp:942-1016`, `Unit.cpp:10457-10614`, `Creature.cpp:2193-2247`): C++ does not leave a killed creature as an alive unit with health 0; it sets `JUST_DIED`, runs creature death-state bookkeeping, and ends in `CORPSE`. Rust now routes lethal global creature-victim melee through represented AI damage state and then calls `set_death_state_runtime(JustDied, game_time_secs)` so corpse/respawn timestamps use the C++ seconds scale instead of the existing `mark_ai_dead(now_ms)` shortcut. Scope remains intentionally partial: rewards, loot, proc hooks, aura/combat cleanup, tapper/group handling, pet notifications, and full `Unit::Kill` parity remain open.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031ak` (global legacy creature melee now covers represented sparring/fake-damage for canonical Creature victims; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `Unit::DealDamage`, `Unit::DoMeleeAttackIfReady`, `Creature::CalculateDamageForSparring`, `Creature::ShouldFakeDamageFrom`, and `HITINFO_FAKE_DAMAGE` (`Unit.cpp:780`, `Unit.cpp:2219-2220`, `Creature.cpp:1726-1765`, `UnitDefines.h:468`): C++ sets the fake-damage hit flag before sending `AttackerStateUpdate`, then clamps actual applied damage inside `DealDamage`. Rust now exposes `HIT_INFO_FAKE_DAMAGE`, lets `AttackerStateUpdate` carry explicit hit-info flags, implements represented creature sparring helpers, clamps canonical creature health at the sparring threshold, and emits fake-damage attack-state packets when already at/below threshold. Scope remains creature-vs-creature: pet victims, full player/charm ownership parity beyond represented control state, absorbs/resists/procs/redirect, death cleanup, loot, rewards, and exact threat/proc side effects remain open.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031aj` (global legacy creature melee now handles canonical Creature victims instead of player-only victims; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `Creature::Update -> Unit::DoMeleeAttackIfReady -> Unit::AttackerStateUpdate` (`Creature.cpp:847`, `Unit.cpp:2103-2130`, `Unit.cpp:2211-2227`): C++ uses generic `Unit* victim` and only adds a `Creature* victimCreature` branch for fake-damage flags; there is no player-only filter. Rust removed the experimental `GlobalLegacy` melee driver's player-only cut for canonical Creature victims, applies represented health damage to `get_typed_creature_mut`, emits a visible `SMSG_ATTACKERSTATEUPDATE` plus represented Unit values update through `RuntimePlan`/`SendIfVisibleLikeCpp`, and keeps player victims on the existing explicit victim-session command rail. Scope is deliberately creature-only: Pet victims remain open because canonical map lacks a typed mutable pet accessor, and full `DealMeleeDamage` parity (absorbs/resists/procs/redirect/fake damage/death cleanup/loot/rewards) remains open. No C++ bug was identified here; this was a Rust port gap.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031ai` (global legacy creature melee now calls the represented C++ LOS hook when canonical attacker state exists; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `Unit::AttackerStateUpdate` and `WorldObject::IsWithinLOSInMap` (`Unit.cpp:2168-2173`, `Object.cpp:1187-1210`): after range/facing and attacker-state gates, C++ rejects base/offhand melee if `!IsWithinLOSInMap(victim)`. Rust now factors a `is_creature_melee_los_clear_like_cpp` helper over `WorldObjectEnvironment`, calls it from the experimental `GlobalLegacy` creature melee apply path when the canonical attacker creature is present, counts `melee_los_rejections`, and consumes the swing like C++'s caller-side `resetAttackTimer` after `AttackerStateUpdate` returns. This is an infrastructure hook, not real VMAP LOS: `SharedCanonicalMapManager` is still fixed to `wow_map::MapManager` with `NoopTerrainGridLoader`, so live production remains permissive until canonical map terrain/VMAP LOS is injectable/real. Focused coverage proves the helper delegates to the environment and rejects when the environment returns false; full global-helper false-LOS integration awaits real/injectable canonical terrain. Non-player victims, offhand/extra attacks, `UNIT_STATE_MELEE_ATTACKING` unification, full canonical Unit attack timers, real VMAP/dynamic LOS, and live server/client smoke remain open.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031ah` (global legacy creature melee now removes C++ attacking-interruptible auras on confirmed hits; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `Unit::AttackerStateUpdate` (`Unit.cpp:2168-2173`): after attacker-state/LOS gates and before melee damage, C++ calls `AtTargetAttacked` and `RemoveAurasWithInterruptFlags(SpellAuraInterruptFlags::Attacking)`. Rust already represented the aura-removal primitive on `Unit`; the experimental `GlobalLegacy` creature melee driver now applies it to the legacy attacker for confirmed canonical hits in the same global tick, counts `attacking_interrupt_auras_removed`, and leaves unrelated interrupt flags intact. Because Rust releases the legacy map lock before canonical player health mutation, the removal occurs after the canonical damage write but before swing commit inside the same tick; full C++ ordering will be revisited when melee ownership is unified on canonical `Unit`. LOS, non-player victims, offhand/extra attacks, `UNIT_STATE_MELEE_ATTACKING` unification, full canonical Unit attack timers, and live server/client smoke remain open.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031ag` (global legacy creature melee now honors represented C++ `Creature::CanMelee`; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `Unit::DoMeleeAttackIfReady` and `Creature::CanMelee` (`Unit.cpp:2085-2101`, `Creature.h:180-181`, `CreatureData.h:58`): C++ returns before victim lookup and before `isAttackReady` when the creature static flag `CREATURE_STATIC_FLAG_NO_MELEE` is present. Rust now propagates `creature_template_difficulty.StaticFlags1..8` through loaded-grid template resolution into `CreatureTemplateLifecycleRecord`/`CreatureLifecycleMetadata`, preserves the static flags across map-owned respawn snapshots, exposes `Creature::can_melee_like_cpp()`, and the experimental `GlobalLegacy` creature melee driver rejects `NO_MELEE_FLEE` before queuing a ready swing. Focused coverage proves DB-backed loaded-grid static flags are retained, respawn snapshots keep static flags, and a no-melee creature produces no swing/damage/command. `UNIT_STATE_MELEE_ATTACKING` remains represented by the transitional `CreatureAiState::InCombat` model; LOS, non-player victims, offhand/extra attacks, full canonical Unit attack timers, and live server/client smoke remain open.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031af` (global legacy creature melee now honors represented C++ charging/casting preconditions before ready swings; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `Unit::DoMeleeAttackIfReady` (`Unit.cpp:2085-2101`): before victim lookup and before `isAttackReady`, C++ returns while `UNIT_STATE_CHARGING`, and while `UNIT_STATE_CASTING` unless the current channeled spell has `SPELL_ATTR5_ALLOW_ACTIONS_DURING_CHANNEL`. Rust now applies those represented preconditions in the experimental `GlobalLegacy` creature melee driver before marking a swing ready, counts `melee_precondition_rejections`, and leaves the represented swing timer ready because C++ exits before `resetAttackTimer`/`setAttackTimer(100)`. Focused coverage proves charging and blocking channel states produce no swing/damage/command, while an allow-actions channel can still hit. `UNIT_STATE_MELEE_ATTACKING` and `Creature::CanMelee` remain explicitly unrepresented in this transitional legacy AI model, and LOS, non-player victims, offhand/extra attacks, full canonical Unit attack timers, and live server/client smoke remain open.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031ae` (global legacy creature melee now gates damage by represented C++ `AttackerStateUpdate` attacker state; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `Unit::DoMeleeAttackIfReady` and `Unit::AttackerStateUpdate` (`Unit.cpp:2085-2170`): after range/facing succeeds, C++ may still return before damage if the attacker is pacified, cannot auto-attack, or has `SPELL_AURA_DISABLE_ATTACKING_EXCEPT_ABILITIES`; the base attack timer is still reset by the caller after `AttackerStateUpdate` returns. Rust now snapshots `Unit::can_attacker_state_update_melee_like_cpp(false)` for the global legacy creature swing, suppresses canonical health mutation and victim delivery when it fails, counts `attacker_state_rejections`, and records the swing as consumed so the represented base timer returns to `2_000` rather than retrying at `100ms`. Focused coverage proves a pacified in-range creature produces no damage/command and cannot immediately retry. LOS, non-player victims, offhand/extra attacks, exact `CanMelee`/casting/channel gates, full canonical Unit attack-timer unification, and live server/client smoke remain open.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031ad` (global legacy creature melee uses C++ 100ms retry timer after represented auto-attack errors; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `Unit::DoMeleeAttackIfReady` (`Unit.cpp:2085-2155`): when the base attack is ready but the represented auto-attack gate returns `NotInRange` or `BadFacing`, C++ does not call `AttackerStateUpdate` and sets the base attack timer to `100`; on a successful attack it resets the base timer to the attack speed. Rust now records failed global creature swings with a 100ms retry cooldown instead of allowing every global tick to retry immediately, and successful global creature swings restore the represented base attack timer from `CreatureCreateData::base_attack_time`. Focused coverage proves a range failure sets `swing_timer_ms = 100` and an immediate second tick produces no swing, while a successful hit restores `2_000`. LOS, non-player victims, offhand/extra attacks, exact Unit attack-timer unification, and live server/client smoke remain open.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031ac` (global legacy creature melee now gates damage by C++ facing/boundary radius; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `Unit::DoMeleeAttackIfReady`, `Unit::IsWithinBoundaryRadius`, and `WorldObject::_IsWithinDist` (`Unit.cpp:2085-2155`, `Unit.cpp:672-678`, `Object.cpp:1057-1083`): after the melee-range gate, C++ rejects auto-attack with `BadFacing` unless the attacker is inside the target boundary radius or has the target inside a 120-degree frontal arc. Rust now applies the same represented gate before canonical health mutation in the experimental `GlobalLegacy` creature melee driver, counts `melee_facing_rejections`, and fixes the shared boundary helper to include attacker/target combat reach like C++ `IsWithinDist`. A focused test proves an in-range but behind/outside-boundary victim keeps health and receives no command; the existing session boundary-radius test still passes after the helper correction. LOS, non-player victims, attack-timer retry semantics, offhand/extra attacks, and live server/client smoke remain open.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031ab` (global legacy creature melee now gates damage by represented C++ melee range; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `Unit::DoMeleeAttackIfReady` and `Unit::IsWithinMeleeRangeAt` (`Unit.cpp:2085-2155`, `Unit.cpp:649-667`): a ready base attack must reject when `!IsWithinMeleeRange(victim)` before `AttackerStateUpdate`/damage, using `max(attackerCombatReach + victimCombatReach + 4/3, NOMINAL_MELEE_RANGE)`. The experimental `GlobalLegacy` creature melee driver now snapshots attacker position/combat reach, checks victim canonical position/combat reach before mutating health, counts `melee_range_rejections`, and emits no victim command when out of range. Existing positive fixtures were corrected to place the victim inside melee range; a new focused negative test proves far victims keep health and receive no command. Facing, boundary-radius, LOS, non-player victims, and timer retry semantics beyond the represented range rejection remain open; no live server/client smoke was run for this slice.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031aa` (global legacy aggro uses map-owned non-player owner leash centers; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `Creature::CanCreatureAttack` (`Creature.cpp:2671-2721`) and `WorldObject::IsWithinDist` (`Object.cpp:1062-1078`, `Object.cpp:1148-1151`): after the dungeon/recent-damage bypasses, C++ uses `GetCharmerOrOwner()` as the leash center and applies `victim->IsWithinDist(owner, dist)`, including both combat reaches. Rust now builds owner snapshots not only from active player candidates but also from map-owned creatures on the same `(map_id, instance_id)` before mutating the aggro scan. Creature-owned creatures can therefore leash against their non-player owner position instead of failing `owner_position_unrepresented`; missing/cross-map owners still fail closed. Focused coverage proves an in-range victim is allowed from the creature owner center and a victim beyond that strict distance is rejected. Normal session-owned runtime remains unchanged; this only affects the gated `GlobalLegacy` experimental owner path and no live server/client smoke was run for this slice.
- `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031z` (global legacy aggro hydrates `TurretAI` spell range gates; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%; honest global stays ~74%) — contrasted against C++ `TurretAI::TurretAI` and `TurretAI::CanAIAttack` (`CombatAI.cpp:190-210`): the first creature spell is resolved through spell info, `_minimumRange = GetMinRange(false)`, `m_CombatDistance = GetMaxRange(false)`, and attack is rejected outside max range or inside non-zero min range. Rust now passes `SpellMiscStore` + `SpellRangeStore` into `LegacyCreatureAggroConfigLikeCpp`, wires the real loaded DB2 stores from `world-server`, resolves `creature.spells()[0] -> SpellMisc.range_index -> SpellRange`, and feeds the represented `creature_ai_can_attack_like_cpp` TurretAI min/max inputs instead of failing all TurretAI attacks as unrepresented. Missing spell/range data still fails closed and increments `ai_can_attack_unrepresented`. Focused coverage proves too-close and too-far candidates are rejected while an in-range player starts aggro, plus the missing-range fail-closed path. Normal session-owned runtime remains unchanged; this only affects the gated `GlobalLegacy` experimental owner path and no live server/client smoke was run for this slice.

Candidate routing in `crates/world-server/src/main.rs` (`resolve_runtime_event_candidates_like_cpp` + `deliver_runtime_plan_like_cpp`, test-only): NearbyVisible filters is_in_world+map_id+instance_id+distance (2D if required_3d=false, 3D if true); MapBroadcastVisible same minus distance (single DashMap pass, Candidate/SkipReason); ExplicitPlayer reads target map_id/instance_id from the registry entry so the session gate accepts it; SelfOnly is NOT broadcast (self_only_skipped, no owning-session guessing). C++ anchors: WorldObject::SendMessageToSet/InRange (Object.cpp:1746-1764), MessageDistDeliverer (GridNotifiers.h, GridNotifiersImpl.h:43-46). - `RUNTIME-SLICE / #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.001` (L3/L4 runtime infra; NO inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%) — ADR step 3: introduced live-runtime tick-ownership infrastructure WITHOUT behavior change. Added `RuntimeTickOwner { Session, GlobalLegacy }` (default Session) + `tick_owner()`/`set_tick_owner()` on the shared legacy `MapManager` (crates/wow-world/src/map_manager.rs), and `RuntimeOutput { packets }` (INITIAL session-local seam, not the final fanout design). Extracted `run_creatures_tick`/`run_combat_tick` (kept SEPARATE; cadences %4/%2 intact) that produce a `RuntimeOutput` plan instead of sending; `tick_creatures_sync`/`tick_combat_sync` are now thin wrappers `run_* + flush_runtime_output`, sending via the same `send_tx` in the same order with identical bytes. Guard in `WorldSession::update`: when the map's owner != Session the two ticks are skipped; the `creature_tick` increment and `tick_auras()` stay OUTSIDE the guard (aura cadence unchanged). `runtime_tick_owner_like_cpp` copies the Copy enum under a short read lock and releases it before any tick. Default stays Session -> behavior identical. Reviewer confirmed byte-identical extraction line-by-line, lock scope, guard scope. Codex conditions integrated (flume bounded send CAN block - not claimed non-blocking; test renamed to skips/zero; RuntimeOutput = initial seam). 6 tests added; wow-world 1057/0 (1051+6), x2. NO world-server/main.rs or wow-map touched (caller global = Slice 4). Checks: `cargo fmt --check`; `PROTOC=/home/cdmonio/.local/protoc/bin/protoc cargo check -p wow-world` / `-p world-server`; `git diff --check`. FOLLOW-UP (Slice 4): anchor the GlobalLegacy-skip test to a real `update()` call (test 4 currently replicates the guard logic); flume fanout needs try_send/queue/out-of-lock; RuntimeOutput needs recipient/session routing for real fanout.
- `FIDELITY / #NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.766` (no inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%) — reordered the gates inside `WorldSession::can_take_quest` to match the exact C++ `Player::CanTakeQuest` order (Player.cpp:14093-14102): disabled -> Status -> ExclusiveGroup -> Race/Class/Level -> Skill -> Reputation -> PreviousQuest -> DependentPreviousQuests -> DependentBreadcrumbQuests -> Seasonal -> Conditions -> Expansion. The incremental insertions from #757-763 had drifted (Seasonal early; dependent cluster before Race/Class/Level). Fidelity fix, not behavior change: pure ANDs, no `msg`/reject-reason path, boolean invariant (suite 1051/0 identical). Becomes a real requirement once `msg=true` `SendCanTakeQuestResponse` is wired. Timed/Day/Week/Month and recursive BreadcrumbQuest remain unported. Docstring updated from stale C# ref to C++ anchor. Checks: fmt; `cargo test -p wow-world --lib` 1051/0 x2; `cargo check -p world-server`; `git diff --check`. 1 file, pure block movement.
- `TEST-DEBT / #NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.765` (no inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%) — cleaned the 9 pre-existing failing tests in `wow-map` (`crates/wow-map/src/map.rs`), taking the crate from 605/9 to **614/0**. All three clusters were TEST bugs (production is faithful to C++); only `#[cfg(test)]` code changed, zero production. (A) 7 `process_respawns_*loaded_grid*` tests loaded the wrong grid (`cell_from_world(0,0)` -> grid (32,32)) while the `respawn_info` fixture declares `grid_id:7` -> grid (7,0); the production gate `is_grid_id_loaded` (map.rs:3285, decode map.rs:12545-12554) therefore took the unloaded branch (deletes timer without respawn, faithful to C++ `Map::DoRespawn` `if(!IsGridLoaded) return`, Map.cpp:2165-2168). Fixed by loading the grid the fixture declares (`cell_from_grid_center(GridCoord::new(7,0))`), asserts unchanged. (B) `update_spawn_group_conditions_condition_failure_*` asserted post-removal state without draining the remove-list; despawn only enqueues (deferred removal, like C++ `Map::DespawnAll`+`RemoveAllObjectsInRemoveList`, Map.cpp:2034-2055,2574). Fixed by calling `remove_all_objects_in_remove_list_like_cpp()` before the removed-state asserts; outcome asserts unchanged. (C) `world_object_los_delegates_*` expected raw orientations 0.25/0.75, but the hit-sphere transform sets orientation to `atan2(0,0)=0.0` (world_object.rs:987-1010) and C++ `WorldObject::IsWithinLOSInMap` discards orientation for the raycast (Object.cpp:1187-1219). Fixed the expected orientation to 0.0; xyz/guids/check_dynamic/!result unchanged. Reviewer independently confirmed all three are test bugs (not masking production bugs). Checks: `cargo fmt --all -- --check`; `PROTOC=/home/cdmonio/.local/protoc/bin/protoc cargo test -p wow-map --lib` (614/0); `PROTOC=/home/cdmonio/.local/protoc/bin/protoc cargo check -p world-server`; `git diff --check`. 1 file, all `#[cfg(test)]`. wow-map baseline is now clean (614/0) ahead of the live-runtime roadmap work.
- `RUNTIME-ROADMAP step 1 / #NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.764` (no inventory change; count stays 736/759 = 96.97%) — executable characterization of the live-runtime engine split, as a regression anchor before any behavior change. ARCHITECTURE FINDING (corrects stale CLAUDE.md): the runtime has THREE coexisting world models — (A) legacy `wow_world::MapManager` (`Arc<RwLock<>>`, main.rs:1620): shared across sessions, runs creature AI/combat via `tick_creatures_sync`/`tick_combat_sync`, but has NO clock of its own (only advances when a logged-in session ticks); (B) canonical `wow_map::MapManager` (main.rs:1641): owns the global tick loop (`spawn_canonical_map_update_loop` main.rs:6179, ~10ms) and a faithful `Map::Update` structure, but its creature update uses `CreatureRuntimeUpdateContext::default()` (manager.rs:430) which builds a plan but has NO `match` that dispatches `AiUpdateTick`/`MeleeAttackIfReady` (map.rs:6286-6295) — i.e. no real AI/combat/fanout; (C) the global loop ticks only (B), never (A). The `WorldSession.creatures: HashMap` field CLAUDE.md mentions no longer exists; `map_manager.rs` is ~3651 lines (not ~890). Two test-only anchors added: `canonical_map_update_visits_creature_with_no_real_ai_combat_effect_like_cpp` (wow-map/manager.rs:3910 — goes through `manager.update()`, asserts the creature is visited [actions_recorded>0] but HP/position are unchanged = no real AI/combat dispatch) and `two_sessions_sharing_legacy_map_manager_see_same_creature_state` (wow-world/session.rs:48217 — two sessions on the same Arc<RwLock<MapManager>> see shared live creature state). INCREMENTAL ROADMAP toward live runtime (anti big-bang; the _attic/ big-bang failed with 176 errors): (2) give the legacy map (A) its own clock — sessionless creature-AI tick; (3) creature-move fanout from the global tick via a per-map session registry; (4) move combat resolution to the global clock (resolve once, not per-session); (5) unify respawn (per-session queue -> canonical); (6) single source of truth for creatures (method-by-method); (7) real SendObjectUpdates + scripts/weather/threat. Steps 2+ carry real architectural risk (combat double-resolution, ordering) and need explicit user go-ahead each. SEPARATE TECH DEBT discovered: wow-map has 9 pre-existing failing tests (7x `process_respawns_*loaded_grid*`, `update_spawn_group_conditions_condition_failure_*`, `world_object_los_delegates_*`). Checks: `cargo fmt --all -- --check`; `cargo clippy -p wow-map -p wow-world --all-targets` (no new warnings); `PROTOC=/home/cdmonio/.local/protoc/bin/protoc cargo test -p wow-map --lib` (605 passed / 9 pre-existing failed); `PROTOC=... cargo test -p wow-world --lib` (1051 passed / 0 failed, x2); `git diff --check`. Zero production change (both anchors are `#[cfg(test)]`-only).

- `#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.759` — represented-partial C++ `Player::SatisfyQuestExclusiveGroup` gate is now enforced at quest accept (not manual-test-ready; no push/install/restart). Source-of-truth contrasted: `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Entities/Player/Player.cpp:14093-14102` (`CanTakeQuest` includes `&& SatisfyQuestExclusiveGroup`) and `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Entities/Player/Player.cpp:15348-15391`. New `satisfy_quest_exclusive_group_like_cpp(&self, quest)` replicates the C++ over session-local stores: returns true for `exclusive_group <= 0`; iterates `quest_store` peers sharing the group (skipping self); rejects when a peer already satisfied its day/week/seasonal cooldown (`!SatisfyQuestDay/Week/Seasonal`), when a peer is active in `player_quests` (`GetQuestStatus != NONE`), or when a non-repeatable-pair peer is rewarded (`!(quest.IsRepeatable() && peer.IsRepeatable()) && GetQuestRewardStatus`). It is called from `can_take_quest` immediately after the SatisfyQuestStatus block (C++ AND order preserved); active vs rewarded are modeled on separate stores (`player_quests` vs `rewarded_quests`) to avoid double-counting; the push path (remote PlayerBroadcastInfo) was left untouched. - `TECH-DEBT/BUGFIX` (no inventory change; count stays 729/752 = 96.94%) — fixed the 3 remaining pre-existing deterministic wow-world test failures, all rooted in the C++ QuestStatus enum (QuestDef.h:140-150: COMPLETE=1, 2 is commented-out/invalid, INCOMPLETE=3). (1) PRODUCTION BUG: the quest-menu helper at `crates/wow-world/src/session.rs:~17498` used `matches!(status.status, 1 | 2)`; `2` is an impossible quest status, so an ender quest in INCOMPLETE(3) never entered the questgiver menu. Fixed to `COMPLETE | INCOMPLETE`, matching C++ `Player::PrepareQuestMenu` (Player.cpp:13981-13984) which adds menu icon 4 for both COMPLETE and INCOMPLETE — this is what auto-opens SendQuestGiverRequestItems for a single incomplete ender. (2)+(3) TEST BUGS: two tests (`combat_tick_kill...`, `represented_player_condition_context...`) used the invalid literal `status: 2` and a `status: 1` meant as in-progress; production was already faithful to C++. Corrected the test data to the named `crate::conditions::QUEST_STATUS_*_LIKE_CPP` constants without weakening any assert. Result: `cargo test -p wow-world --lib` = 1036 passed / 0 failed (deterministic, x2). Checks: `cargo fmt --check`; `PROTOC=/home/cdmonio/.local/protoc/bin/protoc cargo check -p world-server`; `git diff --check`. 1 file touched.
- `TECH-DEBT` (no inventory change; count stays 729/752 = 96.94%) — fixed pre-existing flaky test `process_pending_ticks_non_bomb_trap_target_like_cpp` by making `WorldSession.represented_gameobject_use_states` a `BTreeMap<ObjectGuid, _>` instead of a `HashMap`, so the ~18 `.iter().collect()` collectors that feed the client-observable `represented_gameobject_use_effects` Vec iterate in deterministic GUID order (`ObjectGuid` Ord = high then low; the test's expected order already matched). Not a C++ ordering contract — `represented_gameobject_use_states` is a per-session abstraction (C++ processes simultaneous trap activations via the Map grid with no guaranteed client order). Checks: `cargo fmt --check`; 10/10 deterministic runs of the test; `PROTOC=/home/cdmonio/.local/protoc/bin/protoc cargo test -p wow-world --lib` = 1033 passed / 3 failed (only the 3 pre-existing deterministic failures, zero new); `PROTOC=... cargo check -p world-server`; `git diff --check`.

## Critical Rules

- Source of truth is TrinityCore C++ 3.4.3 build 12340/TDB442 in `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy`.
- Do not use Retail, Cataclysm, 3.3.5, C#, or CypherCore as authority.
- Do not trust existing Rust, tests, comments, prior AI work, or roadmap checkboxes without C++ anchors.
- No live server start, install, restart, push, or merge unless Joe explicitly requests it.
- Commit locally only after independent review plus validation.


- `#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.508` current slice (review `APROBADO`; CI `CI_OK`): bounded correction for represented `GameObject::SendGameObjectDespawn()` session range parity. C++ `WorldObject::SendMessageToSetInRange` constructs `MessageDistDeliverer` without `req3dDist`, so Player distance uses `GetExactDist2dSq`; Rust now gates the visual-despawn helper with `Position::is_within_dist_2d` while preserving #507 guards and session-local visible GUID state. It does not claim full `SendMessageToSet`, phase filtering, shared-vision/m_seer fanout, exact cell traversal, ObjectAccessor/global fanout, scripts/AI/DB/PoolMgr, or manual-test-ready GameObject runtime.

## Progress Estimate

Overall core migration estimate with #522 represented `SendGameObjectDespawn` shared-vision target in-world gate remains conservatively `~94.5%` (do not claim >95% until ownership/runtime gaps below are substantially closed). #522 closes only the bounded session-side guard that canonical typed Player/Creature shared-vision targets must still be `in_world` before acting as represented `viewer->m_seer == target` fanout targets for `SMSG_GAMEOBJECT_DESPAWN`; it does not add a new runtime/manual-test milestone. The latest #505-#522 work lets logged-in `WorldSession::process_pending()` consume map-owned GameObject/DynamicObject update evidence through represented session packets under increasingly C++-anchored receiver gates, without claiming full runtime/global fanout.

This remains intentionally below the R8 TSV row-completion ratio because heavy runtime ownership gaps remain. ObjectAccessor has typed map-local read seams over canonical `MapObjectRecord` for several families plus a limited typed Player global-registry seam, `Map::RemoveAllObjectsInRemoveList` represents switch-list and deferred remove-list drains, supported Creature/GameObject loaded-grid respawn inputs have bounded map-owned seams, and `ManagedMap::update` now consumes #449 represented DynamicMapTree head update before instrumentation/later phases, represented DynamicObject, AreaTrigger, Creature, Conversation, SceneObject, GameObject, #439 Transport visitation, #440 MultiPersonalPhaseTracker expiry helpers, #442 live Creature/GameObject/AreaTrigger move-list drains, #443 represented `ProcessRelocationNotifies` source gating/marked-cell consumption, and #444 represented `DelayedUnitRelocation` visibility/AI plan exposure before reset, plus #445 represented `SendObjectUpdates` update-flag/mask consumption, #446 represented script-schedule processing, #447 represented map-owned weather timer/default-weather update/reset before personal phase, #448 represented tail ScriptMgr/metrics boundary, #450 represented DynamicMapTree insert/remove/contains model-key facade, #451 exact GameObject add/remove consumption of explicit model evidence, #452 represented `CreateModel()` map-object flag plus `EnableCollision(toggledState)` evidence, #453 represented `GameObject::UpdateModel()` model-key refresh over canonical exact typed GameObject records, #454 locally represents the map-owned `GameObject::SetDisplayId -> UpdateModel` caller order seam, #455 locally represents `GameObject::SetGoState -> EnableCollision` caller order/evidence, #456 adds the represented map-owned `SetLootState` collision/restock caller seam, #457 consumes it from represented loot release, #458-#460 consume linked-trap/ClearLoot/GOOBER pre-branch ordering inside `GameObject::Update`, #461 adds the bounded non-consumed chest/goober post-`ClearLoot()` early return. These are still narrow represented/runtime-dependency slices, not full live parity: ObjectAccessor/live AddToWorld/fanout/scripts/visibility, several runtime loaders, creature vehicle templates, GameObject AI/go-type/per-player/loot/model/collision/visibility/transport behavior, Transport AI/scripts/spline/teleport/passenger/static-passenger/fanout runtime, exact TypeContainerVisitor/order/cell traversal, real terrain/vmap/dynamic-tree collision/BIH/LOS/intersections/heights, real GameObjectModel geometry/model-store hydration, Transport delayed-add model runtime, live `SetDisplayId()`/`SetGoState()` caller consumption outside these facades, DB save/delete execution for runtime branches, full relocation notifier side effects/visibility/fanout/runtime, full personal-phase visibility/fanout/runtime, real script command execution/script manager hooks/destruction count integration, real WeatherMgr/weather regeneration/player-count/fanout/script-hook runtime, and broader Unit/Player inventory/auras/threat/motion/update-field work remain open.

Manual test point: no new client-facing manual milestone from #410/#421/#423/#425/#426/#427/#428/#429/#430/#431/#438/#439/#440/#442/#443/#444/#445/#446/#447/#448/#450/#451/#452/#453/#454/#455/#458/#459/#460/#461/#462/#463/#464/#465/#466/#467/#468/#469/#470/#471/#472/#473/#474/#475/#476/#477/#478/#479/#480/#481/#482/#483/#484/#485/#486/#487/#488/#489/#490/#491/#492/#493/#494/#495/#496/#497/#498/#499/#500/#501/#502/#503/#504/#505/#506/#507/#508/#509/#510/#511/#512/#513/#514/#515/#516/#517/#518/#519/#520/#521/#522. #522 is still represented session-side receiver gating; it does not make GameObject/DynamicObject runtime manual-test-ready.

- `#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.522` review `APROBADO`; CI `CI_OK`; validation OK; committed locally before this docs-only handoff refresh; no push/install/restart.

C++ quirk is preserved: if Player already has another viewpoint or removal mismatches, Player state/request booleans stay unchanged but DynamicObject `_isViewpoint` still toggles once typed DynamicObject plus bound typed Player caster are present. Missing DynamicObject, absent bound caster, or bound caster missing/not Player returns an honest no-mutation outcome and does not toggle the DynamicObject.

- `#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.403` (review `APROBADO`; focused checks passed; committed locally in the current #403 HEAD; no push/install/restart)
  - Moves loaded-grid Creature resolver GUID ownership to the C++ caller point: `resolve_loaded_grid_creature_like_cpp(spawn_id, map_object_guid)` consumes the map-generated runtime GUID, validates high type and map id, and propagates it into `CreatureCreateLifecycleRecord`, `Creature`, and optional `MapObjectRecord::Creature` instead of storing a GUID in spawn metadata or deriving one from `spawn_id`.

## Remaining Gaps / Next Dependency

`#NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.405` corrects only the DB-backed data/builder dependency for the pure Creature loaded-grid resolver/loader closure; it does **not** complete full live respawn/runtime ownership. Remaining heavy dependencies toward >95% core include:

1. Wire the now-available DB-backed Creature loader inputs into the live world-server loaded-grid `DoRespawn` closure: select the map-owned runtime GUID from the `&mut Map` seam, compose `CreatureLoadedGridLifecycleResolverLikeCpp`, and pass typed `MapObjectRecord::Creature` records into `Map::process_due_respawns_composite_loaded_grid_respawns_like_cpp` without holding map locks across DB/async work.
2. Complete creature vehicle-template runtime beyond #465's represented identity/local-kit seam before treating those spawns as full runtime complete; real VehicleKit seat data/install/uninstall, AddToWorld, ZoneScript, AI, ObjectAccessor/fanout, dynamic tree/collision, packets and DB persistence remain open.
3. Add the equivalent GameObject DB-backed loader bridge/wiring into the same loaded-grid `DoRespawn` seam, preserving #398 lifecycle validation and no fake transport/template fallbacks.
4. Execute real live `PoolMgr` Spawn1Object/ReSpawn1Object/Despawn1Object from the existing action records, including loaded-grid loader invocation, DB respawn save/delete ownership, recursive subpool execution, and deterministic/true RNG integration where required.
5. Complete `AddToWorld`/`RemoveFromWorld`, ObjectAccessor map-local ownership, grid/session fanout, dynamic tree/collision hooks, scripts/AI/vehicle/zonescript initialization, and cleanup side effects after typed records enter/leave `Map`.
6. Continue reducing Player/Unit/Creature/GameObject lifecycle, UpdateFields, inventory/equipment, auras, threat, motion, spawn/despawn/respawn and AreaTrigger runtime gaps.

Recommended next slice after #465: choose the next heavy runtime dependency rather than adding cosmetic helpers. Highest-value options are loaded-grid GameObject DB-backed `Create/LoadFromDB` wiring into the same `&mut Map` respawn seam, or Creature/Vehicle runtime after the represented vehicle-template identity/local-kit seam; both require fresh C++ anchors and honest scoped acceptance, and neither may fake missing AddToWorld/ObjectAccessor/fanout/scripts/AI/zonescript/formation/dynamic-tree side effects as complete.


## #NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.495 — represented typed DynamicObject create visibility

The create block serializes `CreateObject2`, `TypeId::DynamicObject`, Stationary create bits without MovementUpdate, stationary XYZ/O, and create-time `[size][flags=0][ObjectData][DynamicObjectData]` values for caster/type/spell visual/spell/radius/cast time. `wow-world` reads canonical map-owned typed `MapObjectRecord::DynamicObject` data from `wow_map::Map::map_objects` via nearby `dynamic_objects`, filters by map and distance to `represented_visibility_source_position_like_cpp()`, creates packets for newly visible DynamicObjects, emits existing out-of-range updates, and updates `client_visible_guids_like_cpp`.

## #NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.496 — canonical map instance ownership for represented visibility scans

Status: represented only; reviewed/validated and committed locally at `current #496 HEAD`; review `APROBADO`; CI `CI_OK`; no push/install/restart. This fixes canonical map-instance selection for represented GameObject/DynamicObject visibility scans only.

C++ anchors: `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Entities/Player/Player.cpp:25344-25364` (`Player::SetViewpoint(target,true)` uses the player's current world/map context), `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Entities/Player/Player.cpp:25389-25395` (`Player::GetViewpoint()` resolves relative to `*this` through ObjectAccessor/type mask), `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Grids/Notifiers/GridNotifiers.cpp:29-60` (`VisibleNotifier::SendToSelf()` visits objects in the active map/grid container), and `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Grids/Notifiers/GridNotifiers.cpp:113-119` (DynamicObject caster-player visibility refresh is map-local to the live seer).

Implemented Rust seam: `WorldSession::visible_gameobjects_from_canonical_map_like_cpp` and `WorldSession::visible_dynamic_objects_from_canonical_map_like_cpp` now derive the canonical map from `current_canonical_player_map_key_like_cpp()` before scanning. `current_canonical_player_map_key_like_cpp()` searches the whole canonical `MapManager` with `do_for_all_maps(...)` for the typed Player GUID. If the typed canonical Player key exists and its `map_id` matches the requested map id, the helpers use `manager.find_map(key.map_id, key.instance_id)`; if the typed Player key exists for another map id, they return no scan rather than falling back to `(requested_map_id, 0)`; if no typed canonical Player key exists anywhere, legacy/test fallback remains `(requested_map_id, 0)`. DynamicObjects include both represented nearby `world.dynamic_objects` and `grid.dynamic_objects` buckets before typed canonical record resolution.

Focused tests/checks: `cargo fmt --check`; `cargo test -p wow-world canonical_visibility_uses_player_instance --lib -- --nocapture` (2 passed, including `canonical_visibility_uses_player_instance_cross_map_blocks_instance_zero_fallback`); `cargo test -p wow-world dynamic_object_visibility_uses_player_instance --lib -- --nocapture` (1 passed); `PROTOC=/home/cdmonio/.local/protoc/bin/protoc cargo check -p world-server`; `git diff --check`; TSV 9-column check. `canonical_visibility_uses_player_instance` and `dynamic_object_visibility_uses_player_instance` build same-map-id instance `0` and instance `7` canonical records with the session Player typed in instance `7`, proving the represented visibility helpers return only instance `7` GameObject/DynamicObject create data. `canonical_visibility_uses_player_instance_cross_map_blocks_instance_zero_fallback` covers the reviewer case: session/requested map id `571`, typed canonical Player on map `1` instance `7`, and a visible GameObject in `571/0`; the helper returns `None` and does not serialize the instance-0 object.

Remaining gaps: this does not close full `UpdateVisibilityOf`, full `SetSeer`, ObjectAccessor/session fanout, DynamicTree/collision, exact grid traversal, visibility packet parity beyond represented create/OOR state already in scope, scripts, aura runtime, or manual-test-ready farsight. Ownership remains canonical `wow_map::MapManager` / `Map::map_objects`; `WorldSession` only selects an existing managed map and serializes represented first-appearance data.

## #NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.526 — PoolMgr Spawn1Object/ReSpawn1Object loaded-grid caller-loader execution seam

Source-of-truth remains Trinity C++ `Map::ProcessRespawns` and `PoolMgr.cpp`: pooled due timers call `UpdatePool`, pool state is mutated before `Spawn1Object`, Creature/GameObject spawn only occurs on loaded grids, `ReSpawn1Object` despawns then spawns again, and child Pool `ReSpawn1Object` remains no-op/unsupported in this slice.

Implemented Rust seam: `process_due_respawns_composite_loaded_grid_respawns_like_cpp` now passes the existing caller-owned `LoadedGridRespawnRecordsLikeCpp` loader into pool `SpawnOne`/`RespawnOne` actions. `wow-map` still mutates only map-owned `pool_data`, respawn timers, `map_objects`, by-spawn indexes and AddToMap/remove paths; it does not fabricate DB-backed Creature/GameObject records or read DB/template/runtime stores. Loader `None` or loader returning `None` preserves blocked/load-plan evidence, unloaded grids skip without loader calls, missing spawn metadata keeps `pool_spawn_actions_missing_spawn_data`, and loader records use best-effort pre-add records with the primary `AddToMap` deciding success/failure. On primary AddToMap failure, the pooled trigger timer remains removed and pool state is not reverted, matching C++ state mutation before `AddToMap` failure.

Pool respawn-time removal stays no-op.
