### #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031j21 — WotLK spellclick packet and dispatch seam

Status: WotLK-real infrastructure slice for `CMSG_SPELL_CLICK`; not manual-test-ready and not a full `Unit::HandleSpellClick` port.

C++ anchors contrasted: `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Server/Protocol/Opcodes.h:636` maps `CMSG_SPELL_CLICK = 0x3495`; `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Server/Protocol/Opcodes.cpp:934` registers it as `STATUS_LOGGEDIN` / `PROCESS_INPLACE`; `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Server/Packets/SpellPackets.h:903-910` defines `SpellClickUnitGuid` plus `TryAutoDismount`; `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Server/Packets/SpellPackets.cpp:920-924` reads GUID then bit; `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Handlers/SpellHandler.cpp:432-444` resolves an in-world creature/pet/vehicle and delegates to `Unit::HandleSpellClick`; `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Entities/Unit/Unit.cpp:11892-11980` performs the actual spellclick spell/vehicle/AI execution.

Implemented Rust seam: `crates/wow-packet/src/packets/spell.rs` now represents the exact `SpellClick` payload, and `crates/wow-world/src/handlers/spell.rs` registers `ClientOpcodes::SpellClick` as logged-in/inplace with a bounded handler that parses/logs the packet. `crates/wow-world/src/session.rs` now dispatches the opcode instead of leaving it as missing Rust dispatch. This deliberately does not execute spellclick casts yet; the next slice must port the bounded `HandleSpellClick` resolver/executor using the existing `NpcSpellClickStoreLikeCpp`, condition evaluator, spell-cast rail, vehicle seat handling, and AI callback seams.

Validation evidence: focused packet test covers GUID+`TryAutoDismount` read order. Running the C++ dispatch metadata test also surfaced an unrelated Rust drift: `CMSG_USE_TOY` was registered as `ThreadUnsafe` in Rust while C++ `Opcodes.cpp:1007` is `PROCESS_INPLACE`; Rust was corrected to `Inplace`. The same metadata test now explicitly skips the frozen BattlePet client opcodes as modern/non-WotLK exceptions so they do not block WotLK dispatch parity. Remaining gaps: live object lookup as `ObjectAccessor::GetCreatureOrPetOrVehicle`, `IsInWorld` gate, `SpellClickInfo::IsFitToRequirements`, database spellclick conditions at execution time, caster/target/original-caster flag handling, vehicle seat spell validation/basepoint override, real spell cast/aura creation, and creature AI `OnSpellClick`.

### #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031j22 — WotLK spellclick execution plan seam

Status: WotLK-real represented execution-planning slice for `CMSG_SPELL_CLICK`; not manual-test-ready and still not a full `Unit::HandleSpellClick` port.

C++ anchors contrasted: `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Handlers/SpellHandler.cpp:432-444` performs `ObjectAccessor::GetCreatureOrPetOrVehicle`, null/in-world gates, and delegates to `Unit::HandleSpellClick`; `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Entities/Unit/Unit.cpp:11892-11980` evaluates spellclick rows one by one, checks `SpellClickInfo::IsFitToRequirements`, runs `IsObjectMeetingSpellClickConditions`, resolves caster/target/original-caster from `NPC_CLICK_CAST_*`, handles vehicle-seat aura/basepoint overrides, casts or creates fallback auras, then calls creature AI `OnSpellClick`; `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Globals/ObjectMgr.cpp:184-215` defines the simple user-type gates; `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Miscellaneous/SharedDefines.h:989-1008` defines `SPELL_CLICK_USER_*` and `NPC_CLICK_CAST_*`.

Implemented Rust seam: `crates/wow-world/src/session.rs` now has `represented_handle_spell_click_plan_like_cpp`, a bounded plan builder over `NpcSpellClickStoreLikeCpp` plus the existing condition evaluator. It preserves C++ row-by-row continue semantics, maps `NPC_CLICK_CAST_CASTER_CLICKER`, `NPC_CLICK_CAST_TARGET_CLICKER`, and `NPC_CLICK_CAST_ORIG_CASTER_OWNER` into a represented cast plan, marks exact context as unrepresented for party/raid/summon/friend contexts that Rust cannot yet prove exactly, and marks creature AI `OnSpellClick` as unrepresented evidence. `crates/wow-world/src/handlers/spell.rs` now consumes that plan dormantly after parsing `CMSG_SPELL_CLICK`; it logs the bounded plan but still does not execute casts.

Validation evidence: focused tests cover cast-flag mapping and that an unrepresented earlier row does not stop later valid rows, matching the C++ `continue` behavior in `Unit::HandleSpellClick`. Remaining gaps: live `ObjectAccessor::GetCreatureOrPetOrVehicle` lookup including pets/vehicles, true `IsInWorld`, vehicle `GetVehicleKit()->GetCreatureEntry`, seat-id entry path, `SPELL_AURA_CONTROL_VEHICLE` validation and basepoint override, real `CastSpellExtraArgs`/aura fallback execution, true owner GUID for `NPC_CLICK_CAST_ORIG_CASTER_OWNER`, complete party/raid/summon/friend relation gates, spell validation by difficulty, creature AI `OnSpellClick`, and live client validation.

### #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031j23 — WotLK spellclick clicker-cast execution

Status: WotLK-real bounded execution slice for non-vehicle `Unit::HandleSpellClick`; not manual-test-ready and not full spellclick parity.

C++ anchors contrasted: `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Entities/Unit/Unit.cpp:11892-11980` resolves `caster`, `target`, and `origCasterGUID` from each spellclick row and, for `seatId == -1`, calls `caster->CastSpell(target, spellEntry->Id, CastSpellExtraArgs().SetOriginalCaster(origCasterGUID))`; vehicle seat handling remains a separate branch. Rust's current live spell rail emits `SpellGo` from the session player, so it is faithful only when C++ also resolves `caster == clicker` and `origCasterGUID == clicker->GetGUID()`.

Implemented Rust seam: `crates/wow-world/src/session.rs` now exposes `execute_represented_spell_click_plan_like_cpp`, and `crates/wow-world/src/handlers/spell.rs` calls it after building the represented plan. The executor runs only rows where the represented caster is `Clicker` and original caster is `Clicker`, targets either the clicker or clicked creature, and routes through the existing represented spell execution rail. Rows requiring `caster == clickee`, owner original-caster, owner target, vehicle seat spell mods, or creature AI remain explicit skipped/unrepresented counts instead of being faked as player casts.

Validation evidence: focused tests cover a `caster=clicker,target=clickee` spellclick row applying represented spell damage to the clicked creature and emitting `SpellGo`/`UpdateObject`/`CooldownEvent`, plus a `caster=clickee` row being skipped with no packets until real creature-caster spell execution exists. Remaining gaps: live creature/pet/vehicle lookup and `IsInWorld` gate, full owner original-caster GUID propagation, `caster == clickee` execution, vehicle `seatId` path, triggered vehicle flags, aura fallback for `!IsInMap(caster)`, spell difficulty validation, AI `OnSpellClick(spellClickHandled)`, multi-session fanout, and live client/bot validation.

### #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031j24 — WotLK spellclick in-world target gate

Status: bounded C++ handler guard parity for canonical spellclick targets; not manual-test-ready and not full `ObjectAccessor` parity.

C++ anchors contrasted: `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Handlers/SpellHandler.cpp:432-444` first resolves `ObjectAccessor::GetCreatureOrPetOrVehicle`, returns if missing, then returns again if `!unit->IsInWorld()` before delegating to `Unit::HandleSpellClick`. Rust still resolves through the represented canonical map snapshot rather than full `ObjectAccessor`, but the snapshot now carries `Object::is_in_world`.

Implemented Rust seam: `crates/wow-world/src/session.rs` now treats canonical creature/pet records that exist but are not in world as non-clickable for represented spellclick visibility and returns an empty spellclick execution plan, so no represented cast can fire on a removed/out-of-world target.

Validation evidence: focused spellclick test inserts a canonical creature record without `add_to_world()`, then asserts spellclick visibility is hidden, the execution plan is default/empty, execution outcome is default, and no packets are emitted. Remaining gaps: full `ObjectAccessor::GetCreatureOrPetOrVehicle` search semantics across creature/pet/vehicle, vehicle `seatId` entry path, creature-caster spell rail, owner original-caster, AI callback, multi-session fanout, and live client/bot validation.

### #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031j25 — WotLK spellclick owner original-caster bridge

Status: bounded `NPC_CLICK_CAST_ORIG_CASTER_OWNER` execution bridge for the exact owner-is-clicker case; not full original-caster parity.

C++ anchors contrasted: `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Entities/Unit/Unit.cpp:11909-11913` resolves `caster`, `target`, and `origCasterGUID`, using `GetOwnerGUID()` when `NPC_CLICK_CAST_ORIG_CASTER_OWNER` is set. `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Entities/Unit/Unit.h:1135` shows `GetOwnerGUID()` is backed by summoned-by owner data, not by the clicker unless the clickee is actually owned by that player.

Implemented Rust seam: `crates/wow-world/src/session.rs` now carries owner GUID evidence in the represented spellclick snapshot, sourced from canonical Pet owner data or the represented Unit control owner. `execute_represented_spell_click_plan_like_cpp` now executes owner-original-caster rows only when the owner is the current clicker/player, which is equivalent to the existing player-caster spell rail. Missing or different owner GUIDs remain explicit `skipped_unrepresented_original_caster` outcomes.

Validation evidence: focused spellclick tests cover `ORIG_CASTER_OWNER` executing when the clicked creature owner is the current player and skipping with no packets when the owner is another player. Remaining gaps: original-caster propagation for non-clicker owners, true creature/pet caster spell rail, full `GetOwnerGUID` lifecycle population for all summon types, vehicle `seatId` path, AI callback, multi-session fanout, and live client/bot validation.

Validation evidence: `cargo test -p wow-world represented_spellclick_accepts_vehicle_guid_as_creature_or_vehicle_like_cpp --lib`. Remaining gaps: real `VehicleKit()->GetCreatureEntry()`, vehicle triggered flags, seat-id validation, `SPELL_AURA_CONTROL_VEHICLE` basepoint override, passenger lifecycle, creature/pet caster rail, AI callback, multi-session fanout, and live client/bot validation.

### #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031j28 — WotLK spellclick clickee-caster self damage

Status: bounded creature-caster execution for one non-vehicle `Unit::HandleSpellClick` branch; not a full `Unit::CastSpell` port.

C++ anchors contrasted: `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Entities/Unit/Unit.cpp:11909-11963` resolves `caster = this` when `NPC_CLICK_CAST_CASTER_CLICKER` is absent, `target = this` when `NPC_CLICK_CAST_TARGET_CLICKER` is absent, then calls `caster->CastSpell(...)` for non-seat spellclick rows. `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Spells/SpellEffects.cpp:496-549` applies `SPELL_EFFECT_SCHOOL_DAMAGE` only to a live unit target and accumulates direct damage.

Implemented Rust seam: `crates/wow-world/src/session.rs` now executes the narrow represented case `caster == Clickee`, `target == Clickee`, original caster is the clicker or the clicker-owned clickee, and the spell is direct `SPELL_EFFECT_SCHOOL_DAMAGE`. It emits `SMSG_SPELL_GO` with the clicked creature GUID as caster and applies health/update state to the clicked creature without using the player-caster `apply_damage` path, so no false player tap/loot/XP side effects are introduced.

Validation evidence: `cargo test -p wow-world represented_spellclick --lib` now covers `represented_spellclick_executes_clickee_caster_self_damage_like_cpp`, asserting creature health drops, `SpellGo` is emitted with the creature GUID as both caster GUID fields, and an `UpdateObject` follows. Remaining gaps: creature-caster target=clicker/player damage, pet/vehicle caster execution beyond the same represented row shape, non-damage/aura effects, full spell bonus/resistance/proc model, vehicle seat/basepoint/fallback aura path, AI `OnSpellClick`, multi-session fanout, and live client/bot validation.

### #NEXT.RUNTIME.L3.031j29 — WotLK spellclick clickee-caster clicker target

Status: bounded `caster == this,target == clicker` execution for one direct-damage SpellClick branch; not full player-target spell damage parity.

C++ anchors contrasted: `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Entities/Unit/Unit.cpp:11909-11963` resolves `caster` and `target` independently from `NPC_CLICK_CAST_CASTER_CLICKER` and `NPC_CLICK_CAST_TARGET_CLICKER`, then calls `caster->CastSpell(target, ...)` for non-seat rows. `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Spells/SpellEffects.cpp:496-549` is still the bounded effect anchor for direct `SPELL_EFFECT_SCHOOL_DAMAGE`.

Implemented Rust seam: the represented creature-caster SpellClick rail now also handles rows where the clicked creature casts direct school damage at the current clicker. It emits `SMSG_SPELL_GO` with the clicked creature GUID as caster and the player GUID as target, updates the current session's represented health plus the current canonical player health seam, and sends `SMSG_HEALTH_UPDATE`.

Validation evidence: `cargo test -p wow-world represented_spellclick --lib` now includes `represented_spellclick_executes_clickee_caster_damage_to_clicker_like_cpp`, asserting the clicker's health drops, canonical current-player health mirrors it, `SpellGo` keeps the creature caster GUID, and `HealthUpdate` follows. Remaining gaps: full spell hit/miss/resist/absorb calculations, player combat log/proc/threat side effects, non-current-player delivery/fanout, pet/vehicle caster execution beyond this represented row shape, vehicle seat/basepoint/fallback aura path, AI `OnSpellClick`, and live client/bot validation.

### #NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.788 — Ready Check party/group

Status: represented-partial party/group ready-check runtime; not manual-test-ready and not full group category/timeout parity.

C++ anchors contrasted: `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Handlers/GroupHandler.cpp:553-576` resolves `GetPlayer()->GetGroup(packet.PartyIndex)`, returns on missing group, gates `CMSG_DO_READY_CHECK` to leader/assistant, and lets `CMSG_READY_CHECK_RESPONSE` call `Group::SetMemberReadyCheck` without leader/assistant gate. `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Server/Packets/PartyPackets.cpp:375-416` and `PartyPackets.h:396-450` define the bit/byte parser order and server writer order. `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Groups/Group.h:56` defines `READYCHECK_DURATION 35000`, and `Group.cpp:1445-1545` owns `m_readyCheckStarted`, `m_readyCheckTimer`, offline member false responses, member `readyChecked`, completion and reset.

Implemented Rust seam: `wow-packet` now parses `DoReadyCheck` and `ReadyCheckResponseClient` with the exact C++ bit order and writes `ReadyCheckStarted`, `ReadyCheckResponse`, and `ReadyCheckCompleted`. `wow-network::GroupInfo` now owns represented `ready_check_started`, `ready_check_timer_ms`, and member `ready_checked` state, emits C++-ordered ready-check events, marks missing `PlayerRegistry` members as offline/no-session with false response events, and completes/reset when all members are checked. `wow-world` registers/dispatches both CMSG handlers, gates start to leader/assistant only, fans out events through connected `PlayerRegistry` send_tx entries, and avoids await under the mutable group guard.

Validation evidence: focused unit tests cover packet parse/write order, state no-op before start, offline false response/completion order, member response completion, start permission gate, and connected-only fanout. `Group::UpdateReadyCheck` timeout expiry is now a real runtime tick: `GroupInfo::update_ready_check_like_cpp(diff_ms)` decrements the timer each shared tick, calls `end_ready_check_like_cpp` on expiry (resetting `ready_check_started`/`ready_check_timer_ms`/all `ready_checked`, emitting exactly one `Completed`), and `tick_all_group_ready_checks_like_cpp(registry, diff_ms)` iterates all active groups without holding locks during fanout. `world-server` spawns `spawn_group_ready_check_tick_loop` using the existing `map_update_interval_ms`, serializes `ReadyCheckCompleted` via the same packet writer, and participates in `tokio::select!` shutdown. Unit tests cover: NOOP when not started, decrement without completion when time remains, expiry resets all state and emits `Completed`, and exact-zero boundary. Remaining gaps: `PartyIndex` is parsed but represented as current-group only (no full BG/BF/original-group category resolution), no DB writes by design, and no live client/bot/manual-test validation.


### #NEXT.R8.ENTITIES.789 — represented CMSG_SET_ROLE / CMSG_INITIATE_ROLE_POLL

Status: represented-partial group LFG role-change and role-poll runtime; not manual-test-ready and not full BG/BF/original-group category parity.

C++ anchors contrasted: `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Server/Packets/PartyPackets.cpp` and `.h` define `CMSG_SET_ROLE`, `SMSG_ROLE_CHANGED_INFORM`, `CMSG_INITIATE_ROLE_POLL`, and `SMSG_ROLE_POLL_INFORM` wire order; `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Handlers/GroupHandler.cpp:309-331` resolves `GetPlayer()->GetGroup(packet.PartyIndex)`, returns when old role equals new role, broadcasts `RoleChangedInform` before calling `Group::SetLfgRoles`, and sends only to caller when no group exists; `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Groups/Group.cpp:1426-1434` makes `Group::SetLfgRoles` return for absent slots and call `SendUpdate()` after mutating an existing member; `/home/server/woltk-trinity-legacy/src/server/game/Handlers/GroupHandler.cpp` role-poll handler requires a group and leader/assistant before broadcasting `RolePollInform` without state mutation.

Implemented Rust seam: `wow-packet` now represents the exact parser/writer order; `GroupInfo::set_lfg_roles_like_cpp` returns whether an existing member slot was mutated; `wow-world` dispatches both CMSG handlers. `handle_set_role` preserves the C++ `RoleChangedInform` fanout, mutates only an existing member slot, and then emits `PartyUpdate` through `send_party_update(&group, &registry, vra)` only when `SetLfgRoles` actually changed an existing target. An absent target still broadcasts `RoleChangedInform` when old/new differ, but does not mutate and does not emit the `SendUpdate`/`PartyUpdate` fanout. `handle_initiate_role_poll` requires group plus leader/assistant and does not mutate state.

Validation evidence: focused packet tests cover `SetRole`, `RoleChangedInform`, `InitiateRolePoll`, and `RolePollInform` order; focused world tests cover no-group caller-only behavior, oldRole==newRole no-op, existing-target mutation with `RoleChangedInform` followed by `PartyUpdate`, absent-target broadcast without mutation or `PartyUpdate`, role-poll permission rejection, and leader/assistant fanout. Remaining gaps: `PartyIndex` is represented as current group only (no full BG/BF/original-group category resolution), fanout is based on `GroupRegistry`/`PlayerRegistry` rather than full ObjectAccessor/sWorld runtime, no DB persistence by design, no install/restart/server launch, and no live client/bot/manual-test validation.
